rulings.Case Title: DICK FRIEND VS. BAKER HUGHES OILFIELD OPERATIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication issues one to four is based on alleged insufficient evidence of exposure to asbestos in products manufactured or supplied by defendant. The motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of proof. Defendant has not negated plaintiff's deposition testimony of exposure to asbestos in defendant's products while repairing the products. (See e.g. plaintiff's depostion 16, 18, 21, 56, and 343.) Defendant's challenge the persuasiveness of the testimony but that is not sufficient to shift the burden of proof. The Combs declaration is insufficient to establish that defendant never made such products; at most, it creates a triable issue of fact. The motion for summary adjudication on the issue of punitive damages is granted. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RODGER A. HARRIS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: RODGER A. HARRIS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied. Central to the motion is the scope of the complaint. Defendant may be correct that the complaint does not specifically allege para-occupational exposure. However, in plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses, plaintiffs' set forth this theory and the basis for it. As a consequence, defendant was on notice and given those circumstances, the Court may grant leave to amend to conform to proof. As a consequence, defendant did not shift the burden of proof and the declarations of Gosney and Hatfield also create a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RODGER A. HARRIS et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied. Defendant has not shifted the burden of proof based on the testimony of Rodger Harris at 727-732. Even if the burden shifted, the declarations of Mr. Gosney and Mr. Hatfield create a triable issue of fact on exposure. Defendant's reliance on the general state all facts interrogatory is not sufficient to shift the burden of proof on the issues of summary adjudication because the focus of the question is exposure, not the other issues. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID DURETTE SR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Decedent testified to the use of defendant's product as did his son. The Declaration of Duncan does not demonstrate that plaintiff cannot prove its case; rather, the record is equivocal. Defendant's special interrogatories are from 2005 and do not require that plaintiff address the issue that some but not all of defendant's products contain asbestos. For that reason, the Court finds that defendant did not shift the burden of proof. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ARNOLD REGNER JR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on the factually devoid discovery. The Anderson Declaration creates a triable issue of fact on the issue of exposure. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the 3rd and 18th causes of action and punitive damages. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD WORTHLEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT DANA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's discovery responses and decedent's deposition testimony demonstrate that plaintiff possesses evidence of a single exposure to defendant's product. The declaration of Gary Austin demonstrates that Victor made thousands of gaskets and seals, only some of which contained asbestos. Special interrogatory responses 2, 4, 8, 12 and 19 demonstrate that plaintiff has no evidence that the Victor product identified by decedent contained asbestos. Nor is there any basis for concluding that plaintiff has the ability to present such evidence. Plaintiff does not present evidence that establishes a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: GLENNA WHINERY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT CHAMPION INDUSTRIAL CONTRACTORS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant has shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses and the Champion Declaration. Plaintiff's objections the declaration are overruled. Plaintiff fails to present evidence that raises a triable issue of fact. The Cohen Declaration fails to provide a basis from which a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendant knew or should have known of the risks described. The Cohen declaration makes reference to substantial scientific knowledge on the issue but it is silent as to the level of knowledge in the construction industry as a whole or specifically mechanical contractors. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GLENNA WHINERY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery responses. Decedent was not on the premises and plaintiffs have failed to present evidence that raises a triable issue of fact of any duty owed to decedent. The Cohen declarations fail to provide a basis from which one could conclude that defendant was aware of the risks or assumed a duty of care to decedent. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GLENNA WHINERY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication is denied. The motion is based on decedent's lack of exposure to asbestos from defendant's boilers. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. The Tracy declaration does not establish that the boiler could have reached the Contadina site through another source. Jim Whinnery's deposition establishes exposure and decedent's deposition establishes par-occupational exposure. Plaintiff also presents declarations of Jim Whinnery and Ken Cohen that establish a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to evidence are overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: BROADWAY PROPERTIES LLC VS. WE PARK, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 8, 2008, line 18. DEFENDANT WE PARK, INC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend only to join Powell Partners as an indispensable party. The Court may take judicial notice of case #08-625613 and the Court's finding that both Broadway Properties and Powell Partners must be parties to any action seeking to evict We Park while the partners are engaged in an arbitration over control of their respective interests in this property. =(302/PJM/VC)
